rules.description: Malformed or implausible CVE identifiers. v1's pattern fired on every valid CVE (the CVE[^0-9] alternation matched the hyphen); this tokenizes CVE-like strings, validates ^CVE-YYYY-NNNN+$ with no upper digit bound, and range-checks the year against 1999..current+1.

positive: The malware probably exploits CVE-9999 in the wild.
negative: CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) and CVE-2025-8088 are both confirmed with vendor advisories.

rules.description: Hex runs of 16..128 chars whose length matches no standard digest (32/40/64/128). v1 checked only 30/33/50 and missed the 34-char hash in its own sample. URLs and dashed identifiers (UUIDs) are excluded.

negative: SHA256 7f83b1657ff1fc53b92dc18148a1d65dfa13514a2f6b9f9f1c6d1e6f5d1d1c28 and MD5 d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e are well-formed.

rules.description: IPv4 indicators with octets out of range, defang-aware.
ATT&CK referenced without any T/TA technique ID anywhere in the document.

positive: The campaign maps to ATT&CK across the board.
negative: The campaign maps to ATT&CK T1059.003 and TA0002.

rules.description: Onion addresses whose label length matches neither v2 (16) nor v3 (56).
Impossible timeline claims: confirmation or compromise promised for a far-future year. v1 flagged any mention of any year past 2030, including legitimate projections.

positive: The breach will be confirmed by 2035.
negative: Support for the platform ends in 2035 per the vendor roadmap.
